name: London Ambulance Service
description:
The London Ambulance Service dispatches ambulances in emergencies. 
    The key goal is to allocate an available ambulance for every call that can reach the scene within 11 minutes. 
    The entire dispatch process must not exceed a set maximum time limit.

    To make this work, the computer system (CAD) analyzes incident forms and assigns vehicles. 
    It is vital to track the exact location of moving ambulances. 
    This requires staff to follow standard routes and correctly communicate departure and destination, 
    allowing radio operators and the CAD to record data accurately.
highLevelGoals: The system must prioritize the allocation of ambulances to ensure compliance with the critical response time of 11 minutes for emergency calls.
The system should ensure that the entire dispatch process is completed within a predefined maximum time limit to enhance operational efficiency.
Implement a system that provides real-time tracking of ambulance locations to optimize response times and route management.
Ensure effective communication channels are established between radio operators and ambulance drivers to coordinate dispatch operations efficiently.
Enable emergency call handlers to efficiently receive and process calls to initiate the dispatch process without unnecessary delays.
Provide tools and processes for incident form inputters to accurately input data into the CAD system for effective processing and record-keeping.
Ensure that system administrators can effectively maintain the CAD system to guarantee its proper functioning and reliability during operations.
lowLevelGoals: This API call enables dispatch operators to view which ambulances are available and how quickly they can reach the emergency scene.
This API call allows dispatch operators to assign the nearest available ambulance to an incoming emergency call, ensuring timely response.
This API call provides a mechanism for dispatch operators to monitor the time spent from receiving the call to dispatching the ambulance, ensuring adherence to time limits.
This API call enables ambulance drivers to view their current location and the location of the emergency scene for efficient navigation.
This API call facilitates communication between radio operators and ambulance drivers to ensure they receive timely updates during operations.
This API call provides a user interface for emergency call handlers to quickly enter information about the emergency call to initiate the dispatch process.
This API call allows incident form inputters to check for errors or inconsistencies in the data before it is recorded in the system.
This API call enables system administrators to access performance data to ensure the CAD system is functioning optimally during operations.
This API call allows dispatch operators to be notified of any issues that may cause delays in the ambulance dispatch process, enabling them to take corrective actions.
This API call enables ambulance drivers to communicate their current status back to the dispatch system, improving coordination and response tracking.
